questions.question_text: How old was Maya in the plot, Expert detectives?
questions.explanation_text: Maya was a ten-year-old girl who was always inquisitive about her neighbour Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: Who was called Seven in the plot, Expert Detectives?
questions.explanation_text: Nishad was called Seven by his sister Maya for his name meant the seventh note on the musical scale.

questions.question_text: How was Nishad potrayed to be in the plot, Expert detectives?
questions.explanation_text: Nishad as a child was kind and considerate. He gave Mr. Nath a bar of chocolate as he assumed that he was starving. He also refused to call him a crook and decided to befriend Mr. Nath for he believed that he was a lonely soul.

questions.question_text: Mamma's clinic was located at ________. (Expert Detectives)
questions.explanation_text: Mamma's clinic was located at Girgaum where Seven (Nishad) went alone one day and got upset to see Mr. Nath.

questions.question_text: What best describes the children's attitude towards Mr Nath?
Maya talks about the Hyderabadi housebreaker to show that

options.label: B
options.text: Mr Nath could be a criminal.

options.label: D
options.text: There are more criminals around than we imagine.

questions.correct_answer: Mr Nath could be a criminal.
questions.explanation_text: Mr Nath could be a criminal.

questions.question_text: Who considered Mr. Nath culprit?
questions.explanation_text: Maya had a strong opinion that Mr. Nath was a crook and calls him a criminal who lived in recluse.

questions.question_text: Where was Mr. Nath residing?
questions.explanation_text: Mr. Nath had been living at Shankar House for over a year and he was Maya and Nishad's mother's patient.

questions.question_text: Why did Maya and Nishad get an unexpected holiday?

options.label: C
options.text: Due to the death of their principal

options.label: D
options.text: Due to some repair work in their school

questions.explanation_text: The roads were flooded because of heavy rains and the children could not reach their schools, so, they got an unexpected holiday.

questions.question_text: What best describes Mr Nath?
What did Nishad offer Mr. Nath?
questions.explanation_text: Nishad gave Mr. Nath a bar of chocolate for he concluded from Mr. Nath's lean and thin appearance that he was starving.

questions.question_text: What important information did Ramesh give to Nishad?

options.label: A
options.text: That Mr. Nath had a visitor on Sunday

options.label: B
options.text: That Mr. Nath had accumulated wealth

options.label: C
options.text: That Mr. Nath was a criminal

options.label: D
options.text: That Mr. Nath was a miser

questions.correct_answer: That Mr. Nath had a visitor on Sunday
questions.explanation_text: Ramesh told Nishad that Mr. Nath had a visitor every Sunday. It was so because every Sunday he carried two lunches to Mr. Nath's room and the same visitor was there with him each time.

questions.question_text: Who considered himself/herself right? (Expert Detectives)
questions.explanation_text: Seven and Maya argue over the idea of Mr. Nath being an escaped crook. Then Maya puts forward a question that what was the point of all these inquiries if he's not a crook? She was completely assured that her opinion was correct.

questions.question_text: Why did Nishad and Maya get a holiday?
What did Nishad give Mr. Nath? Why?
What is strange about Mr. Nath's Sundays?
What did Nishad want to know about Mr. Nath?
What was Nishad's impression when he saw Mr. Nath?
Where did Mr. Nath live? For how long had Mr. Nath been living there?
Who is the narrator of the lesson Expert Detectives?
Why did Maya believe that Mr Nath had hidden money?
What was Maya's view about Mr. Nath?
How did Mr. Nath get the scars?
How many facts does Maya note down in her list on paper about Mr. Nath?
What was the condition on which Nishad agreed to co-operate with Maya?
Nishad found out about Mr. Nath from ________.
questions.explanation_text: Nishad found out about Mr. Nath from Ramesh. He went down to the restaurant where Ramesh worked and talked to him.

questions.question_text: The mother had told the children about the scars on the face of Mr. Nath. They were ________.
questions.explanation_text: Maya thought that the scars on Mr. Nath's face were caused by his encounter with the police. But Nishad's mother had told him that he got burnt badly, so he had burn scars on his face.

questions.question_text: Why does Maya think Mr. Nath is a crook? Who does she say the Sunday visitor is?
Does Nishad agree with Maya about Mr. Nath? How does he feel about him?

What does Nishad find out about Mr. Nath from Ramesh?
Arrange the information as suggested below.
What he eats
When he eats
What he drinks, and when
How he pays
Who do you think Mr. Nath is? Write a paragraph or two about him.
What else do you think Nishad and Maya will find out about him? How? Will they ever be friends? Think about these questions and write a paragraph or two to continue the story.
What did Maya think about Mr. Nath's scars?
What surprised Mr. Nath when Nishad first met him?
Maya is a more suspicious person than Seven. Do you agree? Justify your answer.
Why did Nishad quietly walk out of the room?
Seven was generous towards Mr Nath. Justify the statement using evidence from the story.

The word 'tip' has only three letters but many meanings. Match the word with its meanings below.
fingertips – be about to say something
the tip of your nose – make the boat overturn
tip the water out of the bucket – the ends of one's fingers
have something on the tip of your tongue – give a rupee to him, to thank him
tip the boat over – empty a bucket by tilting it
tip him a rupee – the pointed end of your nose
the tip of the bat – if you take this advice
the police were tipped off – the bat lightly touched the ball
if you take my tip – the end of the bat
the bat tipped the ball – the police were told or warned
How did Maya spend her unexpected holiday?
What information did Nishad gather from Ramesh?

The words helper, companion, partner, and accomplice have very similar meanings, but each word is typically used in certain phrases. Can you fill in the blanks below with the most commonly used words? A dictionary may help you.
business ________
my ________ on the journey
I'm mother's little ________
a faithful ________ such as a dog
the thief's ________
find a good ________
tennis/golf/bridge ________
his ________ in his criminal activities

Play detectives with each other. Find a person in your class (or some other acquaintance) to speak to. Find out the answers to the questions given below. Be careful to ask your questions in a polite and inoffensive way. Do not force the person to answer you. Then allow the person to ask you the same questions.
Name?
What newspapers or magazines does the person read?
How long has the person lived at the current address?
What does she/he do during the day, i.e. the daily routine?
What do neighbours and friends say about the person?
Who are his/her visitors and what are his/her eating habits? (You can ask a few others about this.)
What do you think about the person?

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
Nishad looked thoughtful. I'd like to find out why he's so thin and why so lonely. I want to know why he doesn't have any friends and lives alone.
"Try to understand, Seven," I told him, "If he's lived in Shankar House for a year and hasn't made a single friend, there's something wrong. He's obviously scared that someone will recognize him and give him up to the cops."
What was going on in Nishad's mind?
What did he decide to find out?
What explanation did the narrator give about Nath's loneliness?
Use the phrase give up in a sentence of your own.
Find the word from the above passage that means the same as afraid.

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
As we walked back towards the clinic Seven said, He doesn't look anything like a monster, Maya. But did you see how thin he is? Maybe he's very poor and can't afford to eat.
"He can't be poor if he's a crook on the run," I told him. He's probably got millions of rupees stashed away somewhere in that room.
"Do you really think he's a criminal, Maya? He doesn't look like one," Nishad looked doubtful.
"Of course he's one, Seven," I said, and he certainly isn't starving. Mr. Mehta told us that Ramesh brings his meals up from the restaurant downstairs.
Name the story and its writer.
Describe the appearance of the man.
What did the narrator and Nishad think about the man?
Who gave them the information about the man ?
Pick out the word from the passage that means the same as perhaps.

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
"Well done, Nishad," I told him. Now that we've made some progress with our inquiries, well have to sort out all the facts like expert detectives so that we can trap the crook.
"How you do go on, Maya," Seven sighed. "How can you possibly imagine he's a crook? He looks so ordinary!" "Criminals can look quite ordinary, smarty," I retorted. "Did you see the picture of the Hyderabadi housebreaker in the papers yesterday? He looked like any man on the street. Nishad looked doubtful.
What were the little children engaged in?
What role did they assume?
What was Nishad's impression of Mr. Nath?
What did the narrator suspect?
Find the word from the above passage that means opposite to doubtful.

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:
I wrote in large block letters: Catching A Crook
Expert Detectives: Nishad and Maya Pandit by Appointment to the whole world.
Then I began writing. About half an hour later, I turned towards Seven who was lying on his tummy, chin cupped in his palms, reading comics. "Want to hear what I've written?" I asked.
He looked up questioningly. "I've listed all the facts we know about Mr. Nath which might help us to trap him," I said. "Want to hear?"
Seven nodded.
"Fact Number 1," I read, "his name is Mr. Nath. We must discover his first name."
"Do you think that's his real name, Maya?" Nishad asked.
"Probably not," I said. "Most crooks have an alias," I added a big question mark after Nath.
Why did the narrator think of writing?
What was Seven doing then?
What had the narrator written about?
Why do the children suspect that his real name is not Mr. Nath?

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions:
As we walked back towards the clinic Seven said, "He doesn't look anything like a monster, Maya. But did you see how thin he is? Maybe he's very poor and can't afford to eat."
"He can't be poor if he's a crook on the run," I told him. "He's probably got millions of rupees stashed away somewhere in that room."
"Do you really think he's a criminal, Maya? U doesn't look like one, Nishad looked doubtful. "Of course he's one, Seven," I said, "and he certainly isn't starving. Mr. Mehta told us that Ramesh brings his meals up from the restaurant downstairs."
Name the story and its writer.
Who does 'he' refer to here?
Who are Maya and Nishad?
How do they guess that he is not starving?
Which phrase in the passage means 'hidden away'?

Read the following passage carefully and answer the questions that follow:
"Well done, Nishad," I told him. "Now that we've made some progress without inquiries; we'll have to sort out all the facts like expert detectives so that we can trap the crook."
"How you do go on, Maya," Seven sighed. "How can you possibly imagine he's a crook? He looks so ordinary!"
"Criminals can look quite ordinary, smarty," I retorted. "Did you see the picture of the Hyderabadi housebreaker in the papers yesterday? He looked like any man on the street." Nishad looked doubtful.
Why was the narrator happy with Nishad?
Why was Seven not ready to believe that Mr. Nath is a crook?
Who is a crook?
Why did the author make mention of Hyderabadi housebreaker?

Read the passage and answer the following questions:
We walked back towards the clinic and Seven said, "He doesn't look anything like a monster, Maya. But did you see how thin he is? Maybe he's very poor and can't afford to eat."
"He can't be poor if he's a crook on the run", I told him. "He has probably got millions of rupees stashed away somewhere in that room."
"Do you really think he's a criminal, Maya? He doesn't look like one," Nishad looked doubtful.
"Of course he's one, Seven", I said, "and he certainly isn't starving. Mr. Mehta told us that Ramesh brings his meals up from the restaurant downstairs."
Name the story and its writer.
Whom did they suspect to be a crook on the run?
What did the narrator and Nishad think about the man?
Pick out the word from the passage that means 'Scary person'.

Read the following passages carefully and answer the questions that follow each of them: (1x5=5)
"Perhaps the police had to set his house on fire to force him out," I suggested. Seven looked unsure.
On the Monday following Mamma's birthday, Seven went alone with her to the clinic at Girgaum as I was spending the evening with a school friend. When they returned, Nishad told me he'd been to see Mr. Nath and I felt most annoyed that I hadn't been there.
Seven had been quite upset about Mr. Nath's gaunt appearance and was sure that he was starving. He told me that he had knocked loudly on Mr. Nath's door that evening and said, "Open the door quickly, Mr. Nath."
What was the profession of the children's mother?
Why did Seven go to the clinic with his mother?
What upsets Nishad when he meets Mr. Nath?
What was it that annoyed the narrator?
Find the word from the above passage that means the same as very thin.
